description: LTX-2.3 22B structural control via the 'LTX-2.3-22b-IC-LoRA-Union-Control' IC-LoRA — canny / depth / pose conditioning from a reference video. You give it a control/reference clip; on Spark it extracts canny edges (CannyEdgePreprocessor), depth (VideoDepthAnything), and pose (DWPreprocessor), and the union IC-LoRA steers generation to follow that structure. TIER-2 / IC-LoRA: rides a model patch + a guide-conditioning path, so it's its own preset (not a stackable catalog LoRA). Graph: Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo example 'LTX-2.3_ICLoRA_Union_Control_Distilled'.
_TEMPLATE: SKETCH / TEMPLATE. The three SHARED LTX-2.3 base weights (dev checkpoint, gemma text encoder, distilled-lora-384) are REPLACE_ placeholders — fill the real HF URLs (same three as ltx2_generate / ltx_hdr). The Union-Control IC-LoRA URL is REAL/verified. RUN --convert-only FIRST: it skips the weight pull and surfaces any missing node classes, which is how you confirm the preprocessor node_packs below are right.
_CONTROL: The graph wires ALL THREE preprocessors (canny 4991, pose 4986 DWPreprocessor, depth 5060/5061 VideoDepthAnything) into the union guide. Which one(s) drive the output depends on the graph's guide wiring; to favor one control type, mute the others or tune the guide via --set after pulling the converted workflow_api.json. Preprocessor MODELS (yolox_l.onnx + dw-ll_ucoco for pose, video_depth_anything_vits.pth for depth) are normally auto-downloaded by their node packs on first run; if a node errors 'model not found', add it to the models list.
_HOWITRUNS: No-build path: public base image, comfy_run.py clones node_packs (incl. the controlnet-aux + depth-anything preprocessors), downloads weights, converts UI->API, applies patches, optionally splices a Tier-1 --lora stack onto the checkpoint (anchor 3940), and renders. Heaviest cold start of the presets (base weights + preprocessor models).

ui.title: LTX-2.3 Control — canny / depth / pose from a reference clip (IC-LoRA)

primary_input.label: Control reference video
primary_input.tooltip: The clip whose structure (edges/depth/pose) the output should follow. Preprocessing happens on Spark.

prompt.default: A knight in ornate plate armor walks through a misty forest at dawn, shafts of warm light cutting through the trees. Slow tracking shot, shallow depth of field, the soft crunch of footsteps on leaves.

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: Describe the OUTPUT scene you want — the structure (edges/depth/pose) is taken from the reference clip, NOT the prompt. Same LTX-2.3 single-paragraph style as generation (shot, lighting, action, camera, audio). (src: Union-Control example workflow + LTX-2.3 prompt guide)

negative.default: pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, ugly

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: Lightricks' shipped LTX-2 negative (same one used across their example workflows).
